title: Aglaia exstipulata
intro: Aglaia exstipulata is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family, and is native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may be beneficial in treating various ailments such as fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Aglaia exstipulata include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Aglaia exstipulata is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of herbal supplements and traditional remedies.
